title: How to sum only positive (or only negative) numbers
task: Total the gains and losses separately from one mixed column.

excel.note: Negatives: =SUMIF(A2:A6,"<0"). With no sum range, SUMIF sums the tested range itself.

explanation: SUMIF with ">0" and no separate sum range totals just the positives: 100 + 250 + 30 = 380; "<0" gives the losses (−50 here). Handy companions: the two always reconcile (SUM = positives + negatives), ABS of the negative total gives losses as a positive number, and COUNTIF with the same criteria counts each side.
